# Day-Scale Loop Observatory v48

The Day-Scale Loop Observatory is a browser-local public demonstration of GoalOS long-loop control. It shows how an agent loop becomes an institution when state is outside the model context and proof gates decide continuation.

## Core idea

A long-running agent loop should not rely on hidden context. It should write a contract, preserve progress, append traces, survive restarts, expose evaluator scores, reduce harness overhead, and record the next bottleneck.

## Public-safe boundary

No account. No forms. No wallet. No token approval. No analytics. No cookies. No browser storage. No network request. No external action. No production authority.

## Output

The page exports a `LongLoopDocket` JSON file containing objective, architecture, virtual days, trace root, gate results, virtual file state, restart records, bottleneck, and claim boundary.

## Relation to GoalOS

The page demonstrates the proof-to-action chain: objective → mission contract → autonomous work → verification → Evidence Docket → governed decision state → action graph → Chronicle → reusable capability.
